qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Indirectly cyclic collections trigger stack overflow in StandardRepresentation
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a recursion-based denial of service in `org.assertj.core.presentation.StandardRepresentation`, while the advisory describes XXE in `org.assertj.core.util.xml.XmlStringPrettyFormatter.toXmlDocument(String)` as reached from `isXmlEqualTo(CharSequence)`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's XML external entity root cause in `XmlStringPrettyFormatter` / `isXmlEqualTo(CharSequence)`. The only final qualifying finding targets a separate stack-overflow denial of service in `StandardRepresentation` collection rendering.
evidence: The advisory JSON describes XXE in `org.assertj.core.util.xml.XmlStringPrettyFormatter.toXmlDocument(String)` and says the affected assertion path is `org.assertj.core.api.AbstractCharSequenceAssert.isXmlEqualTo(CharSequence)`.

The only final qualifying finding, `indirect-collection-cycle-stack-overflow-dos`, reports code locations exclusively in `assertj-core/src/main/java/org/assertj/core/presentation/StandardRepresentation.java` and describes indirect collection-cycle recursion to `StackOverflowError`.

The finding's reproduction harness at `indirect-collection-cycle-stack-overflow-dos/reproduction/Repro.java` exercises `StandardRepresentation.STANDARD_REPRESENTATION.toStringOf(...)` and a failing `assertThat(...).isEqualTo(...)` call; it does not parse XML or reach `XmlStringPrettyFormatter`.
